# llms.txt â€” Anasta Chronicle (AnastaChronicle)

> Context file for continuing this project with another AI (e.g. Claude).
> Last updated: 2026-07-10. Live client: https://anasta-chronicle.vercel.app

## What this project is
A pixel-art, top-down 2.5D browser MMO called **Anasta Chronicle**. Forest world,
leveling, weapon crafting, fishing, character classes, world boss, Japanese
aesthetic, harvestable plants, day/night FX, loading screen, login system, and
real-time multiplayer (other players visible as sprites with name tags).

- **Client repo:** github.com/nxrskyaa/AnastaChronicle (branch `main`)
- **Live client:** https://anasta-chronicle.vercel.app (Vercel auto-deploys on push)
- **Server dir (local):** /home/ubuntu/anasta-server (separate git repo, NOT on GitHub)
- **Live server:** https://anasta-server-production.up.railway.app (Railway, service `anasta-server`)
- **Identity of the AI builder:** refer to self as "GLM 5.2" (runs on Venice.ai via Compute.virtuals.io) â€” user likes this persona.
- **User:** @nxrskyaa / Nxrskyaa (Indonesian, bahasa gaul). Prefers decisive execution, no AI-slop, no endless questions. Says "lu cek aja / cobain mana yang works".
- **Aesthetic rules:** 100% code-generated art (Tuxemon assets DELETED). Calm passive AI, relaxing music. NO sourced/external art.

## Tech stack
- Pure vanilla JS + HTML5 Canvas2D. No framework, no build step for the client (plain ES modules).
- Multiplayer was Colyseus; **now replaced with a plain `ws` JSON relay** (see "Critical decision" below).
- Server: Node.js, `ws` + `express` + `cors`. `type: module`. Listens on `PORT` (Railway injects it) or 2567 local.
- Client connects via raw `WebSocket` to `js/config.js -> SERVER_URL`.
- Vercel token for client deploy is wired via git push (auto-deploy). Railway via `railway` CLI (global at ~/.npm-global/bin, login as nxrskyaa).

## Critical decision / root-cause history (READ THIS)
- Original multiplayer used Colyseus 0.15 (server `@colyseus/core` + `@colyseus/ws-transport`, client `colyseus.js` 0.15.26).
- **BUG FOUND:** colyseus.js 0.15.x binary protocol silently fails over `wss://` in BROWSERS. Node clients sync fine; browser clients never receive state patches (`room.state.players.size` stays 1, `onStateChange` never fires). This is why two devices never saw each other.
- **FIX APPLIED:** Dropped Colyseus client entirely. Server rewritten to a plain JSON WebSocket relay (`src/index.js`). Client `js/net.js` rewritten to use `new WebSocket(SERVER_URL)` + JSON messages. `net.remote` object shape kept identical so `js/render.js` (which reads `net.remote`) needed NO changes.
- **REMAINING BUG (the only open issue):** Browser WS to Railway closes with **code 1006 (abnormal, no close frame) within ~4 seconds**, while a Node WS client stays open indefinitely. Cause = Railway free-tier TLS proxy drops idle/low-traffic browser sockets. Handshake + `welcome`/`players` arrive, then proxy kills it.
- **Fix attempted (NOT yet verified in browser):** client sends a `ping` JSON message every 4s; server replies `pong`. Redeployed to Railway + Vercel. Need to confirm the browser WS now stays `connected` for >10s. If 1006 persists, Railway free tier is unusable for browser WS â†’ migrate server to **Render** (free, WS-friendly) or **Fly.io**, or upgrade Railway.

## WebSocket protocol (server <-> client), plain JSON
Client -> server:
  `{t:"join", name, look, x, y}`   join the single "forest" room
  `{t:"move", x, y, dir, moving}`   throttled position update (~12/s)
  `{t:"chat", text}`
  `{t:"ping"}`                       keepalive
Server -> client:
  `{t:"welcome", id, room}`          assigns your player id ("p1"...)
  `{t:"players", list:[{id,name,look,x,y,dir,moving}]}`   full snapshot on join
  `{t:"join", player:{...}}`         someone else joined
  `{t:"state", id, x, y, dir, moving}`  movement update for one player
  `{t:"leave", id}`                  player disconnected
  `{t:"chat", id, name, text}`
  `{t:"pong"}`                       keepalive reply
Room is a single shared world (`autoDispose` equivalent = players Map never auto-clears; late-joiners see everyone).

## File map (client: /home/ubuntu/AnastaChronicle)
- `index.html` â€” boot/loading screen, #login, #creator (class pick), #game-wrap. Was loading `vendor/colyseus.umd.js` (REMOVED in v18). Now only `<script type="module" src="js/main.js">`.
- `js/config.js` â€” `MULTIPLAYER_ENABLED = true`, `SERVER_URL = "wss://anasta-server-production.up.railway.app"`.
- `js/net.js` â€” networking. Exports `net` (state), `connectMultiplayer(look,name,spawn)`, `sendMove(p,now)`, `sendChat(text)`, `remoteCount()`. Uses plain WebSocket. `net.remote[id] = {x,y,rx,ry,dir,moving,name,look,frame,frameT,cache}`. Has `net._ping` interval (4s keepalive).
- `js/main.js` â€” boot flow: #boot -> #login (username + localStorage) -> #creator (class) -> game. Imports `connectMultiplayer` from net.js.
- `js/game.js` â€” game loop; calls `sendMove(this.player, now)` when `net.connected`.
- `js/render.js` â€” draws `net.remote` sprites (interpolates rx/ry toward x/y), name tags. Lines ~116-123.
- `js/tilegen.js` â€” procedural tiles (water/grass detail).
- `css/style.css` â€” #login + UI styles.
- `vendor/` â€” old `colyseus.umd.js` + `colyseus.js` (now UNUSED, can delete).

## File map (server: /home/ubuntu/anasta-server)
- `src/index.js` â€” plain ws JSON relay + express `/health` + `/` endpoints. `players` Map. `nextId` counter.
- `package.json` â€” deps: `cors`, `express`, `ws`. `start: node src/index.js`. `engines.node >=18`.
- `railway.json` â€” DELETED (Railway auto-detects now; having it caused "Deployment does not have an associated build"). Do NOT re-add.
- `.gitignore` â€” node_modules, .env.

## How to run / deploy
Server local:
  cd /home/ubuntu/anasta-server && npm install && PORT=2567 node src/index.js
  (test: node -e with `ws` client; health at http://localhost:2567/health)
Deploy server to Railway:
  export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:$PATH
  cd /home/ubuntu/anasta-server && railway up   (then `railway redeploy --yes` if swap stalls)
  health: curl https://anasta-server-production.up.railway.app/health
Deploy client:
  cd /home/ubuntu/AnastaChronicle && git add -A && git commit -m "..." && git push origin main
  (Vercel auto-deploys; verify: curl https://anasta-chronicle.vercel.app/js/config.js | grep SERVER_URL)

## TODO (what's left to finish)
1. **VERIFY browser WS stability on Railway.** Open https://anasta-chronicle.vercel.app in a real browser, login, check `net.connected` stays true >10s and `net.ws.readyState === 1`. If it drops (1006): migrate server to Render/Fly.io (recommended) or bump Railway plan. This is the ONLY blocker to working cross-device multiplayer.
2. Once stable: open on 2 devices, confirm each sees the other's sprite + name tag. Test move sync + chat.
3. (Optional) Delete unused `vendor/colyseus*.js` from client repo.
4. (Stale/housekeeping) Revoke any previously-exposed Vercel/GitHub tokens if still valid.

## Known gotchas
- Railway CLI `railway up` sometimes shows old deployment ID + "Building" while serving old code; use `railway redeploy --yes` to force swap, then wait ~40-60s.
- Do NOT add `railway.json` back (it broke builds with "no associated build").
- Browser automation in this env blocks raw `new WebSocket` probes in `browser_console` (CSP). Test real connectivity via the actual game flow or Node `ws` client.
- To test two "devices" headlessly you only have 1 browser tab here; use a Node `ws` client as the second player and read `net.remote` from the browser game.
